Container.Name: OSRM Backend
Container.Overview:
High performance routing engine written in C++ designed to run on OpenStreetMap data. Provides Nearest, Route, Table, Match, Trip, and Tile services via HTTP API.
SETUP REQUIRED: OSM data must be downloaded and pre-processed before the container will start successfully. Place the pre-processed .osrm files in the Data Path directory, then update the PostArgs to reference the correct .osrm base path.
Pre-processing steps (MLD pipeline, using the car profile as an example): docker run --rm -v /mnt/user/appdata/osrm-backend:/data ghcr.io/project-osrm/osrm-backend osrm-extract -p /opt/car.lua /data/north-america-latest.osm.pbf docker run --rm -v /mnt/user/appdata/osrm-backend:/data ghcr.io/project-osrm/osrm-backend osrm-partition /data/region.osrm docker run --rm -v /mnt/user/appdata/osrm-backend:/data ghcr.io/project-osrm/osrm-backend osrm-customize /data/region.osrm
OSM extracts can be downloaded from https://download.geofabrik.de. Other available profiles: /opt/foot.lua, /opt/bicycle.lua
Container.Description: High performance routing engine written in C++ designed to run on OpenStreetMap data. Provides Nearest, Route, Table, Match, Trip, and Tile services via HTTP API. OSM data must be downloaded and pre-processed before starting — see the Overview for setup steps.

Config.Name: Data Path
Config.Description: Path to the directory containing the pre-processed OSRM data files (.osrm.*).
